description: This phase explores childhood experiences (ages 0-12) to understand patterns in family, learning, social dynamics, and emotional development that shape who the user is today.

categories.name: Family Environment & Attachment
categories.description: Understanding the home environment, family dynamics, and early attachment patterns

questions.direct_question: What is your earliest memory of home?
If you could describe your childhood home in one word, what would it be?
questions.purpose: Revealing emotional atmosphere and core feeling
questions.scenario_hook: Think about the feeling you get when you remember being home as a child.

questions.direct_question: Who spent the most time with you during your childhood?
questions.purpose: Identifying primary attachment figures and caregiving patterns
questions.memory_hook: Remember your daily routine when you were little.

questions.direct_question: How would you describe the atmosphere in your childhood home?
questions.purpose: Understanding family dynamics and emotional environment
questions.sensory_hook: Think about the feeling of walking through your front door after school.

questions.direct_question: Who made you feel safest as a child?
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when you were scared or hurt.

questions.direct_question: Who influenced your behavior the most during childhood?
questions.purpose: Revealing influential role models and early imprinting

questions.direct_question: Did you feel emotionally close to your parents or caregivers?
questions.purpose: Assessing emotional bonding and attachment quality
questions.memory_hook: Think about what it felt like to be with your parents.

questions.direct_question: When you cried, who usually comforted you?
questions.purpose: Identifying support systems and emotional regulation patterns
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when you were really upset as a child.

questions.direct_question: Did you feel understood as a child?
questions.purpose: Assessing emotional validation and being heard
questions.reflection_hook: What did it feel like to have people listen to you?

questions.direct_question: Who did you live with most of the time?
questions.purpose: Understanding family structure and living situation
questions.memory_hook: Think about who was usually at home when you woke up.

questions.direct_question: Did you grow up in a joint family or nuclear family?
questions.purpose: Understanding extended family influence and support

questions.direct_question: Which family member influenced you most?
questions.purpose: Revealing key relationships and values transmission
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when a family member taught you something meaningful.

questions.direct_question: Did your family spend quality time together?
questions.memory_hook: Think about the best times you spent with your family.

questions.direct_question: Were family meals common in your home?
questions.purpose: Assessing family connection and sharing rituals
questions.sensory_hook: Think about what dinnertime felt like in your home.

questions.direct_question: Did adults listen to your opinions as a child?
questions.purpose: Assessing validation and feeling of being heard
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when you said something important and someone listened.

questions.direct_question: Did you feel safe at home?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what it felt like to be at home at night.

questions.direct_question: Did you enjoy spending time at home?
questions.sensory_hook: Think about a typical weekend at home when you were little.

questions.direct_question: Were there frequent arguments at home?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what it felt like when family members disagreed.

questions.direct_question: Did your family encourage your interests?
questions.memory_hook: Think about something you were really excited about as a child.

questions.direct_question: What is your strongest family memory from childhood?

categories.name: Learning & Curiosity Patterns
categories.description: Understanding early learning experiences, curiosity, and cognitive development

questions.direct_question: What subject or topic made you the most curious as a child?
questions.scenario_hook: Imagine being in a classroom where you could learn anything you wanted.

questions.direct_question: Were books common in your house?
questions.purpose: Assessing early literacy environment and stimulation
questions.sensory_hook: Think about what you remember seeing around your childhood home.

questions.direct_question: Did someone regularly read stories to you?
questions.purpose: Assessing early literacy experiences and bonding
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when someone read you a story.

questions.direct_question: What kind of stories do you remember hearing?
questions.purpose: Understanding what captured the user's imagination
questions.story_hook: Tell me about the stories that stayed with you.

questions.direct_question: Were you encouraged to ask questions as a child?
questions.purpose: Revealing intellectual nurturing and curiosity support
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when you had many questions about something.

questions.direct_question: Did adults explain things patiently to you?
questions.purpose: Assessing quality of learning support and intellectual safety
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you really wanted to understand something.

questions.direct_question: What fascinated you the most as a child?
questions.purpose: Revealing natural interests and passion areas
questions.scenario_hook: Imagine being completely free to explore anything.

questions.direct_question: Did you enjoy learning before school became serious?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when learning felt like play.

questions.direct_question: Were you encouraged to learn independently?
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when you taught yourself something.

questions.direct_question: Before starting school, were you known for talking a lot?
questions.purpose: Understanding early verbal and social expression
questions.memory_hook: Think about what people said about you when you were very young.

questions.direct_question: Did you enjoy asking 'what', 'where', and 'who' questions?
questions.scenario_hook: Imagine being a young child discovering the world for the first time.

questions.direct_question: Did you frequently ask 'why' and 'how' questions?
questions.purpose: Revealing deeper curiosity and analytical inclination
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when you asked 'why' over and over again.

questions.direct_question: Were you good at explaining things for your age?
questions.purpose: Assessing early communication and cognitive skills
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you helped someone understand something.

questions.direct_question: Did adults consider you curious because of the questions you asked?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what adults said about your curiosity.

questions.direct_question: Did you enjoy telling stories to others?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when you told a story that made others laugh or listen.

categories.name: Play, Imagination & Exploration
categories.description: Understanding play patterns, creativity, and how the user explored the world

questions.direct_question: What did you spend most of your time playing with?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a typical day of play when you were young.

questions.direct_question: Did you prefer outdoor play or indoor play?
questions.sensory_hook: Think about what made you happiest — being inside or outside.

questions.direct_question: Were you encouraged to explore freely?
questions.purpose: Assessing freedom vs. constraint in childhood
questions.memory_hook: Remember a time when you explored somewhere new or interesting.

questions.direct_question: Did adults participate in your games?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when an adult played with you.

questions.direct_question: Did you often invent your own games?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a game you created when you were young.

questions.direct_question: Did you spend more time alone or with others?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you usually spent your free time.

questions.direct_question: Were you physically active as a child?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what your body loved to do when you were young.

questions.direct_question: Did you enjoy building or creating things?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about something you built or created.

questions.direct_question: What activity could keep you engaged for hours?
questions.purpose: Revealing flow states and natural passions
questions.scenario_hook: Imagine having a whole day to do whatever you loved most.

questions.direct_question: What game or activity made you the happiest?
questions.purpose: Identifying pure joy and authentic play
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when you felt pure happiness as a child.

questions.direct_question: How did you spend most of your free time?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what you were doing on a typical weekend.

questions.direct_question: Did you enjoy reading?
questions.memory_hook: Think about whether reading was something you looked forward to.

questions.direct_question: What hobby excited you the most?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about something you were completely obsessed with.

questions.direct_question: Did you watch TV regularly?
questions.purpose: Understanding media influence and screen time
questions.memory_hook: Think about what you remember watching on TV as a child.

questions.direct_question: What games did you play most often?
questions.purpose: Revealing social, competitive, or creative tendencies
questions.story_hook: Tell me about the games you and your friends played.

categories.name: Social Dynamics & Friendships
categories.description: Understanding social patterns, friendships, and belonging in childhood

questions.direct_question: Did you make friends easily?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you met the friends you had when you were growing up.

questions.direct_question: Did you have one best friend or many friends?
questions.purpose: Understanding friendship style and social preferences
questions.memory_hook: Think about your friendships when you were young.

questions.direct_question: What role did you play in your friend group?
questions.purpose: Revealing leadership, support, or followership patterns
questions.story_hook: Tell me about what you were like with your friends.

questions.direct_question: Were you usually the leader or follower in games?
questions.memory_hook: Think about the games you played with other children.

questions.direct_question: Did you trust friends easily?
questions.purpose: Assessing trust and vulnerability in relationships
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you trusted a friend.

questions.direct_question: Did you enjoy group activities?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt in groups of children.

questions.direct_question: Were you included by others?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when you wanted to join in.

questions.direct_question: Did you ever feel left out?
questions.purpose: Revealing experiences of exclusion and resilience
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt like you didn't belong.

questions.direct_question: Did you maintain long-term friendships?
questions.memory_hook: Think about the friendships that lasted through your childhood.

questions.direct_question: What quality made people want to be your friend?
questions.purpose: Revealing strengths and how others perceive the user
questions.story_hook: Tell me about what you were like as a friend.

questions.direct_question: During primary school, were you considerate of other people's feelings?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when someone was upset and you noticed.

questions.direct_question: If a friend was upset, would you usually try to help them?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when you helped a friend who was struggling.

questions.direct_question: Did you naturally care about how others felt?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when others were hurt or sad.

questions.direct_question: Were you kind toward younger children?
questions.purpose: Revealing nurturing tendencies and protection instincts
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you treated children who were younger than you.

questions.direct_question: Did teachers or family members describe you as helpful?
questions.purpose: External validation of helpfulness and cooperation
questions.memory_hook: Think about what people said about you when you were growing up.

questions.direct_question: Were you known as a generous child?
questions.memory_hook: Think about whether you shared things with others.

questions.direct_question: Did you have at least one close friend?
questions.purpose: Assessing ability to form deep connections
questions.memory_hook: Think about the person you were closest to when you were young.

questions.direct_question: Were friendships easy or difficult for you?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what it was like to build friendships.

questions.direct_question: Did you feel accepted by your classmates?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt in your classroom.

questions.direct_question: Did you ever feel excluded by your peers?
questions.purpose: Revealing experiences of isolation and resilience
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt left out.

categories.name: Emotional Development & Self-Awareness
categories.description: Understanding emotional patterns, self-regulation, and early self-perception

questions.direct_question: As a child, were you mostly cheerful or serious?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what you were like when you were surrounded by friends.

questions.direct_question: Were you comfortable around new people?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when meeting new children.

questions.direct_question: Did you feel lonely often?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt like you were alone.

questions.direct_question: Did you express your emotions openly?
questions.memory_hook: Think about whether you showed your feelings or kept them inside.

questions.direct_question: Did you get upset easily?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you reacted when things didn't go your way.

questions.direct_question: Did you feel accepted by the people around you?
questions.purpose: Assessing sense of belonging and validation
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when you were with others.

questions.direct_question: Were you generally happy during childhood?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what made you feel good when you were young.

questions.direct_question: Did you often feel sad or lonely?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt sad as a child.

questions.direct_question: What made you happiest at that age?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about the thing that made you smile the most.

questions.direct_question: Did you feel emotionally secure?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what it felt like to be you when you were young.

questions.direct_question: Were you sensitive to criticism?
questions.purpose: Assessing emotional sensitivity and impact of feedback
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when someone corrected you.

questions.direct_question: Did you lose your temper easily?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you got really angry as a child.

questions.direct_question: When upset, did you calm down quickly or stay upset for a long time?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt after being upset.

questions.direct_question: Did small frustrations affect you strongly?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you reacted to small disappointments.

questions.direct_question: How did you react when things didn't go your way?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when something you wanted didn't happen.

categories.name: Discipline, Boundaries & Parenting Style
categories.description: Understanding how rules, discipline, and boundaries shaped the user

questions.direct_question: How did adults react when you misbehaved?
questions.purpose: Understanding discipline patterns and parenting style
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you got into trouble as a child.

questions.direct_question: Were rules explained to you or simply enforced?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how adults explained things to you.

questions.direct_question: Were you encouraged to learn from mistakes?
questions.purpose: Revealing growth mindset vs. fixed mindset
questions.memory_hook: Think about how your family handled mistakes.

questions.direct_question: Were punishments common in your childhood?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how consequences worked in your home.

questions.direct_question: Were rewards common for good behavior?
questions.memory_hook: Think about whether good behavior was celebrated.

questions.direct_question: Did adults raise their voice frequently?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what it sounded like at home during disagreements.

questions.direct_question: Were you afraid of disappointing your parents?
questions.purpose: Revealing fear-based motivation and approval seeking
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when you thought you had disappointed someone.

questions.direct_question: Did your parents focus more on discipline or understanding?
questions.purpose: Assessing parenting style and emotional safety
questions.memory_hook: Think about how your parents handled your mistakes.

questions.direct_question: Did mistakes make you anxious?
questions.purpose: Revealing perfectionism and fear of failure
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when you made a mistake.

questions.direct_question: Did you feel safe expressing your opinions?
questions.memory_hook: Think about whether you could share your thoughts freely.

questions.direct_question: Did you generally follow rules?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what kind of child you were when it came to rules.

questions.direct_question: Did adults consider you obedient?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what adults said about your behavior.

questions.direct_question: Did you question rules often?
questions.purpose: Revealing autonomy, critical thinking, and authority testing
questions.memory_hook: Think about whether you accepted rules or asked 'why'.

questions.direct_question: Were you known for good behavior?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what people said about your behavior.

questions.direct_question: Did you usually listen to teachers and parents?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you responded to adults in charge.

categories.name: Confidence, Self-Identity & Values
categories.description: Understanding self-perception, values, and identity formation

questions.direct_question: Were you confident speaking in class?
questions.purpose: Assessing academic confidence and public speaking
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when you had to speak in front of others.

questions.direct_question: Were you comfortable meeting new people?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt when meeting someone new.

questions.direct_question: Did new environments make you nervous?
questions.memory_hook: Think about how you felt in a new place.

questions.direct_question: Did you lose confidence easily?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt unsure of yourself.

questions.direct_question: How did you react to unfamiliar situations?
questions.purpose: Assessing coping patterns with new experiences
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when you faced something new.

questions.direct_question: What were you known for between ages 5 and 12?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what people would say about you back then.

questions.direct_question: What did teachers praise most about you?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what your teachers said about you.

questions.direct_question: What did friends appreciate most about you?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what your friends liked about you.

questions.direct_question: What was your biggest childhood strength?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what you were really good at when you were young.

questions.direct_question: What was your biggest childhood challenge?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what was difficult for you as a child.

questions.direct_question: What made you feel confident?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when you felt really sure of yourself.

questions.direct_question: What made you feel insecure?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt uncertain about yourself.

questions.direct_question: What did you want to become when you grew up?
questions.scenario_hook: Imagine someone asking you at age 8 what you wanted to be.

questions.direct_question: Who inspired you most as a child?
questions.purpose: Identifying role models and aspirational figures
questions.memory_hook: Think about who you looked up to when you were young.

questions.direct_question: Did you believe you could achieve your dreams?
questions.memory_hook: Think about whether you believed in yourself.

questions.direct_question: What values were most important to your family?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what your family believed was important.

questions.direct_question: What dream from childhood still exists today?
questions.purpose: Connecting past aspirations to current identity
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a dream that has stayed with you.

questions.direct_question: If someone met 10-year-old you, how would they describe you?
questions.purpose: Revealing core identity and childhood persona
questions.scenario_hook: Imagine a stranger meeting you at age 10.

categories.name: Root-Cause Discovery & Core Memories
categories.description: Understanding formative experiences that continue to influence the user today

questions.direct_question: What is the happiest memory from your childhood?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when you felt pure joy.

questions.direct_question: What is the strongest memory from your childhood?
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a memory that has stayed with you.

questions.direct_question: What made you feel loved as a child?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt truly loved.

questions.direct_question: What made you feel afraid?
questions.purpose: Revealing fear patterns and early anxieties
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you were really scared.

questions.direct_question: What made you feel proud?
questions.purpose: Identifying sources of pride and validation
questions.story_hook: Tell me about a time when you felt really proud of yourself.

questions.direct_question: What made you feel embarrassed?
questions.memory_hook: Think about a time when you felt embarrassed.

questions.direct_question: What did adults praise you for most?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what people said you were good at.

questions.direct_question: What did adults criticize you for most?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what people said you needed to work on.

questions.direct_question: What do you think shaped your confidence at that age?
questions.memory_hook: Think about what made you feel capable or unsure.

questions.direct_question: What part of your childhood still influences you today?
questions.purpose: Connecting past to present and current identity
questions.story_hook: Tell me about something from childhood that still affects you.

follow_up_strategies.if_emotional: That sounds like a meaningful memory. What do you think that experience taught you about yourself?
follow_up_strategies.if_brief: I'm curious about that—what made that so memorable?
follow_up_strategies.if_resistant: That's okay. We can come back to it later if you'd prefer. Let's try another question.
follow_up_strategies.if_reflective: That's a beautiful way to put it. What do you think that says about who you are today?
follow_up_strategies.if_general: That's interesting. Can you tell me more about that?
follow_up_strategies.if_hesitant: Take your time. Sometimes the best answers come when we let them find us.
follow_up_strategies.if_surprising: That's fascinating—I didn't expect that. What do you think made you react that way?
